entry.title: Hana to DB2 connect in RISE

Dear Experts,

I have a scenario where i need some advise.currently customer has onprem ECC in Db2 and additional staging DB2 database to connect with 40 odd interfaces hosted in Azure. This staging server has federated connection with Ecc db2 and has views specific to interfaces. This staging DB2 is using in 2 scenarios 

1- data passthrough using federated ecc db2 connection - specifc interface View is called in staging db2 which will fetch record from ECC db2 and pass it to interfaces. ( no data staging here.)

entry.title: Automating Report Downloads with SAP Build Process Automation from DRC
entry.content:
In the ever-evolving landscape of Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems, efficiency and accuracy are paramount. One such critical process in many organizations is the processing of Document and Reporting Compliance(DRC) Audit Reports. Audit reports contain information that allows the tax authority or an external auditor to detect any anomaly in a company's accounting quickly. These reports are essential for maintaining regulatory compliance, ensuring data accuracy, and making informed business decisions. DRC can create such reports, but downloading them manually can be time-consuming and prone to errors. However, with the advent of SAP Build Process Automation, automating the download of DRC Audit Reports has become seamless and efficient. This blog explores how to leverage SAP Build Process Automation to automate this task, enhancing productivity and reducing operational risks. 

Introduction to SAP Build Process Automation 

SAP Build Process Automation is a robust tool that enables organizations to automate repetitive tasks, streamline workflows, and improve overall efficiency. It integrates seamlessly with SAP's suite of applications, providing a user-friendly interface for designing and deploying automated processes. The key features of SAP Build Process Automation include: 

  • Drag-and-Drop Workflow Design: Simplifies the creation of automation processes without extensive coding knowledge. 
  • Integration Capabilities: Easily connects with various SAP modules and third-party applications. 
  • Scalability: Supports automation at scale, suitable for enterprises of all sizes. 
  • Monitoring and Analytics: Provides insights into process performance and helps in continuous improvement. 

Benefits of Automating DRC Audit Report Downloads 

Automating the download of DRC Audit Reports using SAP Build Process Automation offers several benefits: 

  • Time Savings: Reduces the time spent on manual downloading and processing of reports. 
  • Accuracy: Minimizes the risk of human error, ensuring accurate and consistent data. 
  • Compliance: Ensures timely availability of reports for audit and compliance purposes. 
  • Productivity: Frees up employees to focus on more strategic tasks. 
  • Real-Time Access: Enables real-time access to reports, facilitating quicker decision-making. 

Steps to Automate DRC Audit Report Download 

Step 1: Define the Process 

The first step in automating the download of DRC Audit Reports is to define the process. This involves identifying the key steps involved in the manual process, such as logging into the SAP system, navigating to the relevant module, generating the report, and downloading it. 

Step 2: Design the Workflow 

Using the drag-and-drop interface of SAP Build Process Automation, design the workflow to replicate the manual steps. This typically includes: 

  • Login Module: Automate the login process to the SAP system using secure credentials. 
  • Navigation Module: Create steps to navigate through the SAP interface to reach the DRC Audit Report section. 
  • Report Generation: Automate the generation of the report by specifying the required parameters. 
  • Download Module: Define the steps to download the report and save it to a specified location. 

Step 3: Configure Triggers and Schedules 

Next, configure triggers and schedules for the automated process. Triggers can be set based on specific events, such as the completion of a daily batch job or the arrival of new data. Alternatively, you can schedule the process to run at specific times, ensuring that the DRC Audit Reports are downloaded at regular intervals. 

Step 4: Test the Automation 

Before deploying the automated process, thoroughly test it to ensure that it works as expected. This involves running the process in a test environment, verifying that each step is executed correctly, and checking that the downloaded reports are accurate and complete. 

Step 5: Deploy and Monitor 

Once testing is complete, deploy the automated process in the production environment. SAP Build Process Automation provides tools for monitoring the performance of the automation, allowing you to track its execution and identify any issues that may arise. 

Practical Example: Automating DRC Audit Report Download 

Let's walk through a practical example of automating the download of DRC Audit Reports using SAP Build Process Automation. 
 

 

Step 1: Define the Process 

  • Login to SAP: Access the SAP system using valid credentials. 
  • Navigate to DRC Module: Go to the relevant module where DRC Audit Reports are generated. 
  • Generate Report: Specify the date range and other parameters to generate the report. 
  • Download Report: Save the report to a designated folder on the local system or a network drive. 

Step 2: Design the Workflow 

Using SAP Build Process Automation's workflow designer: 

  • Login Step: Use the "Login" activity to automate logging into the SAP system. 
  • Navigation Step: Use "Navigate" activities to move through the SAP interface to the DRC module. 
  • Report Generation Step: Use the "Generate Report" activity to create the report based on specified parameters. 
  • Download Step: Use the "Download File" activity to save the report to a specified location. 

Step 3: Configure Triggers and Schedules 

  • Daily Trigger: Set up a daily trigger to initiate the process every day at a specific time. 
  • Event-Based Trigger: Alternatively, configure an event-based trigger to start the process when new data is available. 

Step 4: Test the Automation 

  • Run in Test Environment: Execute the workflow in a test environment to ensure all steps work correctly. 
  • Verify Report Accuracy: Check the downloaded reports to confirm they are accurate and complete. 

Step 5: Deploy and Monitor 

  • Deploy Workflow: Move the workflow to the production environment. 
  • Monitor Execution: Use SAP Build Process Automation's monitoring tools to track the process execution and address any issues. 

 

Conclusion 

Automating the download of DRC Audit Reports using SAP Build Process Automation significantly enhances efficiency, accuracy, and compliance in organizational processes. By following a systematic approach to design, test, and deploy the automation, businesses can reap the benefits of reduced manual effort, improved data accuracy, and timely access to critical reports. As organizations continue to embrace digital transformation, leveraging tools like SAP Build Process Automation will be key to staying competitive and efficient in today's fast-paced business environment. 

By automating routine tasks such as the downloading of DRC Audit Reports, organizations can not only streamline their operations but also empower their employees to focus on higher-value activities. This not only boosts productivity but also enhances overall job satisfaction and contributes to a more agile and responsive business.

entry.title: Re: EXTRACTING DATA FROM SAP S/4HANA CLOUD USING THE CUSTOMER DATA RETURN APP AND TRANSFERRING IT
entry.content: Caveat emptor: it is called Customer Data Return for a reason. Not all the data can be extracted- for example customizing tables. Some application tables are also out of scope- user tables like USR21, USR02 and the like. Only data which SAP consider as customer owned can be extracted.

entry.title: Re: Soldoc issue with search by file content
entry.content:
Dear Customer， 

There could be a few potential reasons why the second document is not appearing in the search results when using a t-code. Here are a few things to consider:

- Check the file format: Make sure the second .docx file is not corrupted or saved in a different format that is not compatible with the search function.
- File indexing: It's possible that the soldoc system has not fully indexed the content of the second document, which could be affecting the search results. You may need to wait for the system to fully process the content before it can be properly searched.
- Content visibility: Ensure that the content of the second document is not restricted in any way that would prevent it from appearing in search results. This could be due to permissions settings or other access restrictions.

Best regards,
Vera Jiang

entry.title: Re: Dashboard for daily Basis monitoring
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

The problem you described may be attributed to the data provider and metric configuration. Here are a few things you can consider in troubleshooting this issue:

1. Metric Configuration: Check the metric configuration settings for ABAP_SYS_SHORT_DUMPS_TODAY to ensure that it is set up to capture the correct data for the time period you are interested in. Make sure the time frame is accurately specified for the data you want to view.

2. Data Availability: Verify that the required data for the specific time periods (e.g., 2 days ago or yesterday) is available in the system and is being captured by the data provider. If the data is not being recorded or is not available for older time periods, it may lead to inaccurate values in the dashboard.

3. Data Aggregation: Consider how the data is being aggregated and presented in the dashboard. It's possible that the aggregation method or time intervals in the dashboard settings may not align with your expectations, resulting in incorrect values.

4. Dashboard Filters: Double-check any filters or parameters that are applied to the dashboard. Incorrect filter settings can impact the displayed values.

Best regards,
Vera Jiang

entry.title: Re: Reading multipart form-data in Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Hi ,Can you please try this code 

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message

def Message processData(Message message) {
def body = message.getBody(String)
def boundary = body.split('\n')[0].trim()
def pattern = /(?s)${boundary}.*?Content-Type: text\/plain\s+(.*?)\s+${boundary}--/
def matcher = body =~ pattern

if (matcher.find()) {
def fileContent = matcher.group(1).trim()
message.setBody(fileContent)
}

return message
}

entry.title: Leveraging Automation and AI for Business Excellence and Customer Satisfaction
entry.content:
As we barrel into an AI-heavy world, extensibility and integration are becoming the core functions that help digitize businesses and adapt solutions to meet individual needs. Embracing these functions offers several critical benefits:

  • Gaining a competitive advantage.
  • Simplifying and extending business processes.
  • Building unique processes that stand out from standard software solutions.

In our journey towards achieving business excellence and customer satisfaction, the API Publishing team plays a pivotal role as we publish all digital artifacts for SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP S/4HANA. The team is responsible for publishing digital artifacts with high quality and consistent with customer requirements to fulfil their extensibility and integration scenarios on SAP Business Accelerator Hub. By adopting an automation-driven approach, we streamlined the publishing process, simplified internal workflows, and ultimately benefited our customers. Our published objects support both side-by-side and key user extensibility, ensuring a versatile and adaptive business environment.

Streamlining the Publishing Process

Our approach began with enhancing our publishing tools. We implemented several quality initiatives, tracked meticulously via Jira, and introduced automated review processes using code inspectors. Additionally, we prioritized customer engagement initiatives to align our outputs with customer expectations.

The quality-driven approach began with developing templates and defined formats for the information displayed, based on the belief that "Consistency drives business." Tracking the entire process through Jira allowed us to ensure thorough completeness checks. Automating the review of published artifacts saved significant time and effort, enabling our teams to focus on more strategic tasks. This integration of automation not only streamlined internal processes but also significantly enhanced team efficiency.

Customer-Centric Engagement

We understood the importance of customer feedback and made it central to our process. We planned several customer engagement activities for different artifacts, including documentation. Through usability testing and interviews, we gained valuable insights into customer expectations and identified areas for enhancing the user experience.

These interactions provided a broader perspective, ensuring that all our activities were centered around the customer. The baby steps we took early on have led to substantial results, including more streamlined processes for publishing teams responsible for artifacts used in extensibility and integration.

Leveraging Technology for Efficiency

To support internal teams, we developed a dashboard where artifact owners could view and track the entire publishing process. This visual tool provided transparency and facilitated better management of the publishing workflow.

Moreover, we harnessed the power of Large Language Models to generate descriptions and documentation of these artifacts. This innovation proved to be a win-win for all involved, driving business agility and enhancing extensibility and integration.

Achieving Competitive Advantage and Customer Satisfaction

By leveraging artificial intelligence and automation, we have transformed our business processes, achieving competitive advantage and customer satisfaction. These strategic steps have yielded significant results, such as more efficient publishing processes monitored via visual dashboards and expedited artifact delivery to customers. By embracing AI and automation incrementally, we continue to reap substantial benefits across all facets of our operations, ensuring sustained growth and customer satisfaction in a rapidly evolving market.

In conclusion, our journey of leveraging automation and AI has not only simplified our business processes but also enriched the customer experience. As we continue to innovate and adapt, we remain committed to maintaining our competitive edge and delivering exceptional value to our customers.

#SLIThoughtLeadership @n.prashant.augustine@sap.com @jayprakash.sharma@sap.com

entry.title: Guided Sourcing Auctions
entry.content:
Hi there and welcome to my latest blog entry!

My name is Angeline Gregory, I am one of the Solution Value Advisory Directors covering EMEA/MEE, and I’d like to blog about some of the feedback I’ve experienced while encouraging customers to transition to Guided Sourcing.

Our team has been busy with a global campaign, targeting users of SAP Ariba ‘Classic’ Sourcing who haven't fully explored the potential benefits of Guided Sourcing.

One of the most common questions I’ve received from our customers is how to effectively conduct Auctions using Guided Sourcing.

Let me break this down in my blog post below:

Understanding Auctions with Guided Sourcing

In today's competitive business environment, procurement organizations are constantly seeking ways to optimize their sourcing processes. One effective method is through the use of Auctions, which can be categorized into various types.  This blog will delve into the intricacies of the different Auction types, their benefits, and best practices for successful execution.

Types of Auctions

  1. Reverse Auctions:
    1. The most common type of Auction is where buyers buy goods/services and through a competition between suppliers to drive down the price
    • Suppliers start with initial bids, typically from an RFx round then over the course of the Auction suppliers have the option to bid multiple times to win the business
    • Auctions typically last for 30 mins however, this is dependent on the requirements/complexities – Auctions ‘can’ be automatically extended if bids continue to be received
  2. Forward Auctions:

    Forward Auctions operate similar to Reverse Auctions however, instead of driving down the price which is typical for a Reverse Auction Forward Auctions increase the price – These are amazing for selling materials no longer required by customers, for example redundant IT equipment, scrap metal etc.

    • Buyers (ie suppliers) have the option to submit multiple bids to purchase the goods
    • Example categories are the sale of scrap metals, fleet, used IT equipment
  3. Dutch Auctions:

    In a Dutch Auction in Ariba the customer sets a starting price, a decrement, and a time interval (one minute), and the bid price gradually goes down until participant takes the business or until time is up

    • When a participant decides to bid on a lot then bidding ends immediately – there are no additional bids on that lot
    • It is possible to have a Dutch forward Auction where the buyer sets an increment and the bid price gradually goes up until a participant takes the price.
  4. Japanese Auctions:  In Japanese Auctions the buyer sets a starting price, a price increment, and a time interval.  At each interval the price goes up and participants indicate if they can meet that price or not.  Those participants that cannot meet the price are locked out. 

Benefits of Auctions

  1. Cost Savings:
    The competitive nature of Auctions encourages suppliers to offer their best prices, leading to significant cost savings for the organization.
  2. Efficiency:
    Auctions can drastically reduce the time required to complete the procurement process, making it more efficient.
  3. Supplier Engagement:
    Auctions provide an opportunity to engage with a wide range of suppliers, including those from different geographic locations and with varied capabilities.
  4. Transparency:
    Auctions promote transparency and fairness in the procurement process. All suppliers have equal access to the bidding process, and outcomes are determined based on objective criteria, reducing the potential for bias and favoritism.
  5. Market Insights:
    Auctions provide valuable data and insights into supplier performance, market trends, and pricing dynamics, helping procurement organizations make more informed decisions.

Best Practices for Successful Auctions

  1. Clear Objectives:
    Define clear objectives, whether it's cost savings, supplier diversification, or other strategic goals. Clear objectives will help in evaluating the success of the Auction.
  2. Supplier Pre-Qualification:
    Ensure suppliers are pre-qualified and meet the necessary standards and requirements, we find qualification post-Auction can delay the award of business and can lead to unnecessary confusion.
  3. Auction Format:
    Choose the right type of Auction format. Reverse Auctions are the most common.
  4. Communication:
    Clearly communicate rules, timelines, and expectations to suppliers. Ensure all suppliers have a clear understanding of the Auction process and the criteria for evaluation.
  5. Bid Evaluation Criteria:
    Establish transparent criteria for selecting the winning bid. This can include factors such as price, quality, delivery schedule, and other contractual terms.
  6. Technical Support:
    Ensure technical support is available to both the procurement team and participating suppliers.
  7. Legal and Compliance Considerations:
    Ensure that the Auction process complies with all legal and regulatory requirements, including data privacy, antitrust laws, and any industry-specific regulations.
  8. Post-Auction Negotiations:
    Determine the process for post-Auction negotiations with the winning supplier(s). This can include finalizing contract terms and conditions, as well as discussing any additional requirements – Remember, an Auction will only secure the price, procurement negotiations are required to finalise the contract.

Conclusion

Auctions are a powerful tool for procurement organizations looking to optimize their sourcing processes. By understanding the different types of Auctions, their benefits, and best practices for execution, organizations can achieve significant cost savings, efficiency, and transparency in their procurement activities.

For more detailed insights and a demonstration of Guided Sourcing, feel free to access the following link

Thank you for reading my blog!!

Angeline Gregory

entry.title: Resolving Job Scheduler Issue After Incentive Management Maintenance
entry.content:
Issue Description

During weekly maintenance window for SAP Success Factors Incentive Manager, jobs in the XS Advanced Job Scheduler service would stop working. The logs for the jobs would show the following error:

As a workaround, the node js application in Web IDE was restarted and the jobs would begin working again.

When an application is run via the Web IDE it stays active as long as the current user session is active or expiration of access token. Afterwards, the application endpoints stop being reachable, and this is the cause of error observed above.

This behavior is avoided by deploying the applications to the XS Advanced Runtime environment via MTAR file. The following steps show how to do this.

 

Fix Steps

Web IDE

  • Login to SAP Web IDE for SAP HANA.
  • Switch to the relevant workspace for the application.
     
  • Go to Development </>.

  • Right-click on the project that contains the application → Build → Build.
  • Once complete, open the project mta_archives.
  • Right-click mtar file → Deploy → Deploy to XS Advanced.

     

  • Properties should be pre-populated to:
    • Organization: orgname.
    • Space: same as database name

  • Click Deploy.
  • Wait for deployment success message:

Job Scheduler

  • Login to the Job Scheduler dashboard.
  • Search Instances
  • Click “Open Dashboard”
  • Click on any job named orgname_****.xsjob
  • Take the URL for “Job Action” up to the last forward slash, it will be in the format "https:// orgname-<db_name>-<node_js_name>.<env>.calliduscloud.com":
  • Open support ticket – edit the bold parts accordingly:

HANA JobScheduler <tenant_id> URL Whitelist

Please can the URL "https://orgname-<db_name>-<node_js_name>.<env>.calliduscloud.com" be added to the hosts file and be locally resolvable for the jobScheduler in <tenant_id> (https://jobscheduler-dashboard.<env>.calliduscloud.com ).

This is to resolve the error:

Response: Request Error: Code:-ENOTFOUND, Message:-getaddrinfo ENOTFOUND orgname-<db_name>-<node_js_name>.<env>.calliduscloud.com

  • Once the ticket has been completed, any jobs not beginning with “orgname” should be deleted from Job Scheduler Dashboard  “jobScheduler – Jobs” area.

entry.title: Leveraging Key User Extensibility in BRIM - Transfer data from Billable Items to FI documents
entry.content:
Have you ever wondered how to transfer an information from your incoming billable item to an FI document in your BRIM solution?

In my blog article, I’ll will show you how to extend your BRIM solution using the Custom Fields app in SAP S/4HANA Cloud (Public and Private Edition)!

Content:

  • The sample requirement and solution approach
  • Creation and configuration of the sample custom field
  • Conclusion
  • Appendix

The sample requirement and solution approach

In a BRIM implementation with Convergent Invoicing (CI) and Contract Accounting (FI-CA) it is a common requirement to transfer a custom information from the billable item (BIT) to the item of an FI document.

The Key User Extensibility app Custom Fields allows you to extend your BRIM solution with additional, custom fields and to copy the information from a source to a target object. When you create a custom field using the app, you choose a Business Context, which determines which object is extended. The configuration of your custom fields allows you to enable Business Scenarios, a business scenario controls the automatic transfer of data. The value in the custom field is always copied from source to target, even if the source field is empty.

The diagram below provides an overview of the active Business Contexts and Business Scenarios you need to forward an information from the billable item to the FI document.

Overview active Business Contexts and Business Scenarios

Creation and configuration of the sample custom field

The following section provides basic information on how to make a custom information from your billable item object available in the subsequent Convergent Invoicing and Contract Accounting objects and finally in your ACDOCA document in FI.

1. Launch the Custom Field app (F1481) and create a new field. Choose business context Convergent Invoicing: Billable Items. In our example, the field is labelled MTBitText, it’s of type Text with a length of 20 characters. The identifier is automatically proposed by the app.

Initial field creation

2. The detailed configuration of a custom field allows you to enable several features. In our example, we want to include the new field in the relevant Fiori apps, add it to the API for billable item creation and forward it to the billing document item (Business scenario: Billable Items to Billing Document Item).

Once you have completed the configuration, publish the new field.

The screenshots below show the sample configuration:

Enabled user interfaces

Business Scenario

SOAP API

3. Once the publishing process is complete, you will notice that your field has also been automatically created for the business context Convergent Invoicing: Billing Document Item. Don’t worry, this is the intended feature, not a bug! Since we activated the business scenario Billable Items to Billing Document Item, the app generates the custom field for the target business context as well.

Now proceed with configuring your field across the remaining objects. Start with the context Convergent Invoicing: Billing Document Item and finish with Accounting: Journal Entry Item. Use the diagram from the first chapter as a guide to identify the necessary business contexts and scenarios.

Remember to activate not only the business scenarios, but also the user interfaces. Depending on your specific requirements, you may also need to enable your custom field for OData services and Form Templates.

Remark and warning:

  • You can include additional fields to the total records transferred from FI-CA to the general ledger. To do this, you must activate the Contract Accounting Business Partner Item to Journal Entry Item or Contract Accounting General Ledger Item to Journal Entry Item business scenarios.
  • Think twice if you really need to have the information in FI! If you update totals records with additional fields, the number of totals records and journal entry items may increase considerably.

After completing all the configurations, make sure to publish your custom field for all relevant business contexts.

Business contexts

4. Now it’s time to test the configuration.

4a. Create test billable items that include a value in your custom field.
The new custom field is available on the billable item and the value is shown.

Manage Billable Items

4b. Execute the billing and invoicing process in Convergent Invoicing.

The value of your custom field is copied by the business scenario from source to target.

Manage Billing Documents

Manage Invoicing Documents

4c. Transfer the reconciliation key from Contract Accounting to FI.

As usual, you can customize the grid layouts and include your field in them.

Maintain Reconciliation Key (FPF3)

Manage Journal Entries

Conclusion

From my perspective, the key user extensibility apps offer a powerful yet user-friendly solution for custom extensions in your daily work as a BRIM consultant. Additionally, they help keep the core of your BRIM solution clean, ensuring that each change remains fully compatible with future releases.

Now it’s up to you: Explore the capabilities and features of Key User Extensibility in BRIM!

I hope you find this blog post useful and that you can benefit from this feature in your projects!

 

Appendix

Apps:

  • F1481 - Custom Fields

Documentation:

  • Documentation for F1481-Custom Fields app
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud – BRIM – Extend your system
  • SAP S/4HANA – Convergent Invoicing Extensibility (OP)
  • How to transfer existing fields in a custom include to Custom Fields app

 SAP Convergent Invoicing SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management SAP Contract Accounts Receivable and Payable

entry.title: Safe (and happy) Cycling!
entry.content:
We’ve all become experts at constant movement, whether we’re joining new groups, coaching our friends, or planning our next big adventure.

SAP innovations are delivered in cycles that are familiar to you, putting solutions in your hands that simplify everyday work, increase your effectiveness, and secure your success. Because we know that your finance and HR teams collaborate as they plan and reconcile budgets with employee position and financing requirements, we are releasing continuous improvements to our platform that give you easier, real-time controls. And yes, we've built in AI-powered insights, recommendations, and automation. 

SAP’s public sector solution uses SAP SuccessFactors HCM alongside SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP Business AI, for end-to-end management of strategic, financial, HR and operational decisions for budgeting and forecasting. In the upcoming release cycles (2H2024 and 1H2025) you’ll be able to manage position budget control with all the compliance and automation that you’ve come to expect from your industry-minded neighbors here at SAP. See how people are managed, and if positions are funded, alongside grant reservation and commitments. Look forward to conversations with colleagues that shape policy and efficiently run programs, and make informed, fast financial and operational decisions with live access to intuitive dashboards and simulation tools. Speed along your chosen path to people assignments, finish payroll and accounting runs, and complete the financial reporting of budgets and grants; proactively monitor employee costs, use automated anomaly detection and prevention to mitigate fraud risks and reduce losses. Adopt these features at your own pace.

Subscribe to keep moving ahead: SAP SuccessFactors What's New and SAP S/4HANA Cloud 

It’s a comprehensive and unified approach that increases any organizations’ stewardship of taxpayer money. No matter if your agency has 500 people, 5,000 people, or is larger: we help governments and not-for-profits make data driven decisions that carry their mission forward.

entry.title: Preventing Data Loss During Replication with Partial Data Model
entry.content:
Introduction

This article discusses a strategy for preventing the unintended deletion of reduced XML payloads.

When reduced XML payloads are received from third-party systems like PTC, PDM, Salesforce, etc. SAP's standard behavior is to replace the existing target data objects with the incoming payload content. This means that any fields not included in the reduced payload could be unintentionally deleted in the target data object. This is because SAP interprets the absence of certain fields in the incoming data as an instruction to clear those fields in the existing data object.

Remark: The presence of middleware, such as SAP PI/PO, Seeburger, Jitterbit, etc., between third-party systems and SAP systems, plays a critical role in managing system integration; especially when dealing with different data model structures between the sender and receiving systems.

To mitigate this risk, it's crucial to implement certain strategies. Our strategy here in this particular case is pre-processing. Before processing the incoming XML payload, perform a pre-check to compare it with the currently active data object and decide which fields should be updated and which ones should be preserved.

For your purpose, where product data is being replicated using SAP's standard service provider ProductMDMBulkReplicateRequest_In, handling reduced XML payloads becomes even more critical. This service is designed to handle bulk replication of product data, and it’s essential to ensure that the received payloads are complete enough to maintain data integrity in SAP.

 

Background

When replication involves these third-party systems, the reduced or altered data payloads can lead to unintended consequences, such as missing or incomplete updates in SAP. This is especially critical when the third-party system's data model does not fully align with SAP’s, or when not all data from the third-party system is available or transmitted during replication.

Some key points to consider when integrating a third-party system with SAP:

  1. Data Model Alignment
  2. Handling Incomplete Data
  3. Data Availability
  4. Reduced payload approach: can all fields not required be reduced?
  5. Etc.

Remark: When third-party systems send reduced XML payloads, which might omit certain fields, SAP may interpret these omissions as an instruction to delete or clear those fields. To prevent unintended data loss, it’s important to develop strategies to recognize and handle these cases, ensuring that only the intended updates are made.

Here is an example of a reduced XML payload that might be received from a third-party system:


Figure 1 – An example of reduced XML payload received from a third-party system

More detailed information about the ProductMDMBulkReplicateRequest_In service and the corresponding product data structure in SAP S/4HANA can be found on the official SAP Help Portal. 

 

Problem

When a third-party payload omits certain fields that are already populated in SAP, the SAP system may interpret these missing fields as blank or null during the inbound processing. This can result in previously enriched fields in SAP being overwritten and set to empty if they aren't included in the incoming payload. This behavior underscores the importance of ensuring that critical fields are always included in the payload or implementing logic to preserve existing data in SAP when certain fields are absent.

 

Feasible Option - Abstract

To address the issue of missing fields in third-party payloads leading to overwriting of populated fields in SAP, a Business Add-In (BAdI) can be implemented during inbound processing. Our applied custom logic in this BAdI ensures that fields already populated in SAP are not overwritten with null values if the corresponding fields are absent in the third-party payload. This solution helps maintain data integrity by preserving existing values in SAP when certain fields are not provided in the incoming data.

This article presents a possible solution to address missing data in the incoming payload by using the BAdI MDM_SE_PRD_BULK_REP_REQ. The idea is to detect differences between the active data in SAP and the incoming payload, ensuring that any field missing in the reduced payload is properly identified and managed.

 

Some Nice-To-Have Skills

Here are some nice-to-have skills and knowledge areas for handling this topic:

ABAP programming, T-code: SRT_MONI, Web Services, End-to-End Data Process Flow, XML/XSD, SAP Integration.

 

Solution in a Nutshell

Our idea is to dynamically identify all active fields within the Material Data Model in SAP during inbound processing that are not managed by the third-party system but are populated in the SAP system. We use BAdI MDM_SE_PRD_BULK_REP_REQ for this purpose.

 

Steps to Realize the Solution

For our solution, we pursued the following steps:

  1. Create enhancement implementation for BAdI MDM_SE_PRD_BULK_REP_REQ.
  2. To fill & populate the basic data segment of the XML payload, we created the method FILL_FROM_MARA.
  3. To identify fields in the Material Master (MARA) that are not empty, we used the method cl_abap_struct_utilities=>filled_components. This ABAP class method allows you to determine which components of a structure (in this case, MARA) are populated. By leveraging this, you can focus on fields that are already populated in SAP and ensure that they are preserved and not unintentionally overwritten during data replication or integration processes with third-party systems.
  4. Please see some code samples from method FILL_FROM_MARA. The import parameter is IV_MATNR and there is a changing parameter CS_OUT that holds the XML payload coming from the framework. This takes place in IF_MDM_SE_PRD_BULK_REP_REQ~INBOUND_PROCESSING:

Signature of IF_MDM_SE_PRD_BULK_REP_REQ~INBOUND_PROCESSING:


Figure 2 – Signature of method: IF_MDM_SE_PRD_BULK_REP_REQ~INBOUND_PROCESSING

The code for method IF_MDM_SE_PRD_BULK_REP_REQ~INBOUND_PROCESSING is like this:

 

METHOD if_mdm_se_prd_bulk_rep_req~inbound_processing.

DATA lv_matnr TYPE matnr.

lv_matnr = in-product-receiver_product_internal_id.

me->fill_from_mara(
  EXPORTING
    iv_matnr = lv_matnr " Material Number
  CHANGING
    cs_out   = out ).   " Product Replication Services Structure

COMMIT WORK.

ENDMETHOD.

 

Signature of FILL_FROM_MARA:


Figure 3 – Signature of method: FILL_FROM_MARA

The code for method FILL_FROM_MARA is like this:

 

METHOD fill_from_mara.

    FIELD-SYMBOLS:
      <lv_out>    TYPE any,
      <lv_active> TYPE any,
      <lv_out_x>  TYPE any.
    
    " select the active MARA data
    SELECT SINGLE * FROM mara
      INTO (ls_mara)
      WHERE matnr = _matnr    .      

    " check if it is a change scenario, then process
    IF sy-subrc NE 0.
      RETURN.
    ENDIF.

    " get the fields which are not empty from MARA result str
    cl_abap_struct_utilities=>filled_components(
      EXPORTING
        struct       = ls_mara
      RECEIVING
        filled_comps = DATA(lt_filled_comps) ).

    " assign the related OUT parameters to field symbols
    READ TABLE cs_out-product-mara_tab ASSIGNING FIELD SYMBOL(<ls_mara_out>) INDEX 1.
    READ TABLE cs_out-product-mara_x_tab ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_mara_x_out>) INDEX 1.

    " if related field symbols are not assigned then exit the method
    IF <ls_mara_out> IS ASSIGNED AND
       <ls_mara_x_out> IS ASSIGNED.

      " loop at filled info of MARA fields
      LOOP AT lt_filled_comps ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_filled_comps>).

        UNASSIGN <lv_out>.
        UNASSIGN <lv_active>.
        UNASSIGN <lv_out_x>.

       " assign the field not empty in MARA, for OUT parameter
       ASSIGN COMPONENT <ls_filled_comps>-name OF STRUCTURE <ls_mara_out> TO <lv_out>.

       " if related out field is already have a value, continue. Otherwise take an action
       IF <lv_out> IS ASSIGNED AND <lv_out> IS INITIAL.

         " assign the related fields value of MARA result
         ASSIGN COMPONENT <ls_filled_comps>-name OF STRUCTURE ls_mara TO <lv_active>.

         " if the field has a value in MARA (it has a value because of the loop logic), give it to OUT parameter
         IF <lv_active> IS ASSIGNED AND
            <lv_active> IS NOT INITIAL.

           " need to set update flag
           ASSIGN COMPONENT <ls_filled_comps>-name OF STRUCTURE <ls_mara_x_out> TO <lv_out_x>.

           " only update values which has update flag
           IF <lv_out_x> IS ASSIGNED.
              <lv_out> = <lv_active>.
              <lv_out_x> = abap_true.
            ENDIF.
          ENDIF.
        ENDIF.
      ENDLOOP.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.

 

Conclusion

Integrating third-party applications with SAP can lead to challenges when the payloads are reduced, causing SAP to treat missing fields as blanks or nulls. To address this, we've implemented an inbound BAdI solution that detects and populates missing fields within the XML payload. This approach helps solving the issue of the deletion of populated fields in SAP.

entry.title: Long service award payment to employees as One Time Payment
entry.content:
We got a requirement wherein based on the length of service of employee, a non-recurring pay component should be populated with certain amount automatically on employees profile under Non- Recurring portlet. The amount differs based on the legal entities. Below is the table of the same. So, if an employee completes 1826 days in the legal entity 3 then $500 should be given to that employee, if employee completes 3652 days in the legal entity 1 then $150 should be given and so on.

 

This requirement is not possible via business rules. To meet this requirement, we have created different integration jobs.

We have followed below steps:

  • First, we have configured a new field “Length of service” with data type as “Double” under Employment information.

Please note we must use data type as “Double” else we won’t be able to put the calculation on this field in the subsequent integration jobs.

Please see the below screenshot of the same.

 

 

  • Next, we have created an integration job which will capture length of service based on employee “Original Start date” and updates the Length of service field under Employment information.

This integration job is created with base as “EmpEmployment”. On Length of service field, we have put a calculation which calculates the length in days based on Original start date. 

 

Note: We have put calculation in days because with months we are not getting the round figure. Hence, we used “Days”.

 

 

  • We have created another field which is “Length of your service” as “custom-string3”.

This field is required in the later integrations jobs to put a filter to run the integration jobs.

 According to SAP, apart from standard fields only custom fields with string1,2 and 3 will be available for filters in the integration job. No other custom fields will be available for filer under the integration job from Employment info section

So, we have created this additional field. Below is the screenshot of the field configuration.

 

 

 

  • We have created another integration job which will copy the data from Length of service field (Field which we have created in Step 1) to Length of your service field (Field which we have created in Step 3).

This integration base is also EmpEmployement Info. Below is the screenshot of the calculation which we have put for Length of your service field.

 

 

 

Both the above jobs must run in sequence. The job in Step 2 must run first and then the job which we have created in Step 4.

 

Now, we got the length of service in days based on the above integration jobs.

 

  • Next, we created the last 3 integration jobs for each legal entity as the amounts are different for each legal entity on completion of number of days in company.

Starting entity for this job is “EmpEmployment”. Associate this to OneTimePayments where the pay component should be populated on the employee profile.

  • Under Configure fields, maintain the default value of the pay component. In our case its 7017 (pay component code).Below is the screenshot of the same.

 

              

 

       

  • Issue date field is configured as calculated field, and the calculation will set the issue date as today. i.e. on the day the integration job runs.
  • Amount field is also configured as a calculated field. We have put If/Then statement on this field.

The statement says if say Length of service field value is 3652 then amount value should be 150 for legal entity 1and so on. Below is the screenshot of the calculation:

                             

 

  • Next, we move on to Filter and sort, here we have put filters on legal entity and on number of days. We have put filter on number of days because if we don’t put filter then for employees whose length of service is other than the number of days mentioned above, then amount 0 is populating under One Time Payment section for employees and we don’t want the same. Hence, we put filters. Below is the screenshot of the same:

 

 

 

        Similarly, we have created the integration job for other legal entities and schedule the job at last. So, once the first two jobs ran only then this job must run to get the correct length of service.

Thank you for reading the blog. I hope this will help you in meeting certain requirements. Your feedback, questions, and suggestions are always welcome.  We’d love to hear about your experiences, custom requirements, or any insights you have, so please feel free to share them in the comments section below. Happy Learning 

SAP Integration Suite SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central

entry.title: Integration of DMS and PM for Document Attachment
entry.content:
Introduction

Integrating SAP DMS (Document Management System) with PM (Plant Maintenance) helps companies easily manage and connect important documents to their maintenance tasks, making it simpler for maintenance teams to find the right information, follow rules, and keep equipment running smoothly.

Key Benefits of Integration

  • Improved Access to Documentation: Maintenance personnel can access relevant documents such as technical manuals, safety instructions, and equipment drawings directly from the maintenance work order, reducing time spent searching for information.
  • Enhanced Compliance and Quality: Integration ensures that the latest versions of documents are linked to maintenance orders, helping organizations meet regulatory requirements and maintain high-quality standards in maintenance operations.
  • Streamlined Processes: By linking documents directly to equipment, functional locations, and maintenance orders, the integration streamlines the process of retrieving necessary documentation, thereby reducing delays and improving maintenance efficiency.

Integration Points

  • Equipment and Functional Location Master Data: Documents can be linked to equipment and functional locations in SAP PM. This ensures that all relevant documentation (e.g., technical specifications, operation manuals) is accessible directly from the equipment or location master data.
  • Maintenance Orders and Notifications: Documents related to maintenance tasks, such as maintenance procedures, checklists, and safety instructions, can be attached to maintenance orders and notifications, providing technicians with all necessary information.
  • Work Centers: Documents can also be linked to work centers, ensuring that the personnel working at specific work centers have access to relevant documents.

Technical Aspects of Integration

  • Document Linking: Documents in SAP DMS are linked to PM objects (e.g., equipment, functional locations, maintenance orders) using object links. These links ensure that the documents are readily accessible from the relevant SAP PM transactions.
  • Document Types and Categories: SAP DMS allows categorizing documents into different types (e.g., technical drawings, maintenance manuals). These document types are defined in the DMS and linked to PM objects using object links.
  • Version Control: SAP DMS provides version control, ensuring that the most recent version of a document is available to maintenance personnel. When a document is updated, the new version is automatically linked to the relevant PM objects.
  • Security and Access Control: Security settings in SAP DMS ensure that only authorized users can access sensitive documents. Access control can be defined at the document level to restrict viewing, editing, or printing.

In this blog, let’s discuss how to attach documents to maintenance orders and maintenance notifications.

Steps for Integration Setup (configuration)

1.Define Document Types in SAP DMS:

Set up document types and categories in SAP DMS that will be used for linking with PM objects.

Path: Spro->IMG ->Cross application components -> Document management ->control data ->Define DMS document type ->New entries

Or T. code : DC10

2.Assign Document Types to PM Objects:

Assign the relevant document types to the PM objects. This configuration enables the display of documents in PM transactions.

Path: Spro ->IMG ->Cross application components -> Document management - >control data ->Define DMS document type -> select Document type -> click Define object link

Or T. code : DC10

Similarly, the required object needs to be linked to the document type

3.Assign DMS link Screen to PM Notification:

SPRO -> IMG -> Plant maintenance and customer Service -> Maintenance and service Processing -> maintenance and service notification -> Overview of Notification type ->Select Notification type ->click Screen Structure for Extended Views ->New entries

This setup allows users to conveniently access documents related to their maintenance notifications, streamlining the maintenance process.

Process

Step 1: Create Document, Attach Files, and Link to Object

T. Code: CV01N

Use the 'Object Links' tab to link the document to the relevant objects like equipment, functional locations, Notification and maintenance orders. This ensures that the document is easily accessible whenever the linked object is viewed or processed.

By completing these steps, you effectively integrate the document into SAP, making it available for maintenance and other relevant processes.

Step 2: Display Attached Documents in Maintenance Notifications and Orders

Once documents are attached and linked to maintenance objects, you can easily view them directly from maintenance notifications and orders. Here's how to access and display the attached documents.

1.Notification

2.Order

The attached documents will be displayed under the Additional Data tab in the order

Common Use Cases

  • Preventive Maintenance: Attach maintenance checklists and procedures to preventive maintenance orders to ensure that technicians follow the correct steps.
  • Breakdown Maintenance: Provide troubleshooting guides and technical manuals directly linked to maintenance notifications, aiding in faster resolution of breakdowns.
  • Safety and Compliance: Link safety instructions and compliance documents to equipment or maintenance orders to ensure adherence to safety standards.

By integrating SAP DMS with SAP PM, organizations can significantly enhance their maintenance processes, improve access to critical documentation, and ensure compliance with regulatory standards. The seamless integration helps in achieving more efficient and effective plant maintenance operations.

entry.title: SAP CAP: Understanding of Actions and Functions (Part 1)
entry.content:
In SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAPM), actions and functions play a crucial role in defining the business logic and extending the capabilities of your services. They are used to perform custom operations that go beyond the standard CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) operations provided by the OData protocol.

Actions: Used for operations that modify the system state, handle side effects, and require transaction management.

Functions: Used for read-only operations, optimized for performance, and suitable for complex queries and calculations.

They are further categorized into two types:

Unbound:  actions/functions are like plain unbound functions in JavaScript.

Bound: actions/functions always receive the bound entity's primary key as implicit first argument, like this pointer in Java or JavaScript.

Unbound Example using SAP Standard API:

  • Supplier Invoice API : https://api.sap.com/api/OP_API_SUPPLIERINVOICE_PROCESS_SRV/resource/
  • API reference documentation :

  • EDMX Documentation :In the below metadata file, we can see that the function “Post “ is not bounded on the any entity.

Bound Example using SAP Standard API:

  • Maintenance Order API : https://api.sap.com/api/OP_API_MAINTENANCEORDER_0002/resource/
  • API reference documentation :

  • EDMX Documentation : In the below metadata file, we can see that the function “ApproveMaintenanceOrder“ is bounded on the “MaintenanceOrderType” entity.

Conclusion : Actions and functions play a crucial role in developing robust, scalable, and maintainable applications in SAP CAPM. They enable developers to encapsulate business logic and enhance service capabilities beyond the standard CRUD operations.

In the next blog, I ll explain how we can utilize the existing actions/functions provided by SAP Standard API available on SAP Business Accelerator Hub in SAP CAP Project.

Reference:

  • https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/guides/providing-services#actions-functions
  • https://api.sap.com/api/OP_API_MAINTENANCEORDER_0002/resource/
  • https://api.sap.com/api/OP_API_SUPPLIERINVOICE_PROCESS_SRV/resource/

entry.title: Scheduling & Broadcasting Analysis for Office Reports in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Many organizations are in the process of moving legacy BI workflows to SAP Analytics Cloud, where they are complemented by new workflows for visualization, dashboards, planning and much more.

One notable exception is report scheduling and broadcasting, for SAC stories and applications, and for Analysis for Office (AfO) reports.

As organizations have progressed into SAP Analytics Cloud, they have become fully aware of the constraints that exist for scheduling and broadcasting of SAC reports. These limits fully apply to SAC Stories and Applications, but nowhere are those limitations greater than for Analysis for Office.

SAC’s focus is on creating insights and sharing them within groups, rather than on communicating insights to broader audiences. Many organizations also need to utilize a “push” methodology to distribute and place insights into the paths of those who need them to perform their duties efficiently and effectively.

AfO Scheduling & Report Broadcasting

The continuing popularity of AfO is partly a tribute to the importance of Microsoft Excel as the historical standard in financial analysis, but more importantly it demonstrates that AfO is a convenient and dependable means of providing timely analysis of real-time data. It supplements analytics applications such as SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP BusinessObjects by allowing users to slice and dice information with Excel’s pivot table functionality.

SAP BusinessObjects has provided the primary means for many organizations to push these insights out to information consumers, but maintaining an SAP BusinessObjects deployment for this one purpose is neither practical nor desirable.

Insights don’t exist in a vacuum: they need to be communicated to be effective.

  • BEx Analyzer had BEx Broadcaster.
  • AfO in the pre-Cloud period had SAP BusinessObjects Publications.
  • AfO in the new Cloud environment has ???.

How can such organizations move beyond SAP BusinessObjects?

Moving AfO Beyond SAP BusinessObjects

In analytics, SAP Analytics Cloud is the path forward, and AfO functionality is also following that path. To move fully to the Cloud, organizations need to find an alternative to AfO scheduling and broadcasting using SAP BusinessObjects Publications.

Moving AfO reports to the SAP Analytics Cloud Repository is the first step, making them available to your SAC users. However, to schedule and broadcast these reports, they will have to use a third-party solution such as APOS Publisher for Cloud.

APOS Publisher for Cloud

APOS Publisher for Cloud delivers an efficient scheduling environment that enables users to organize and execute the timely processing of their AfO reports, including time-based report scheduling with hourly, daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly report processing, as well as event-based and conditional report scheduling to meet tactical processing needs.

APOS Publisher for Cloud is simple to deploy and use. Publisher for Cloud’s Web User Interface makes SAC story, SAC application, and AfO report scheduling very simple for all users.

 

Automated and data-driven report broadcasting capabilities allow for filtered and customized report content to be delivered to targeted end recipients for consumption and decision making. The data-driven report filtering capabilities mean that a dynamic process is established to read values from a configured data source and dynamically generate personalized report content. This report broadcasting functionality is a low-maintenance means of producing and distributing user-specific versions of the reports to users. This approach is highly effective in providing focused information to networks of analysts, executives, customers, suppliers, store managers, site supervisors, sales teams, or any other such related information consumers.

Moving Forward

While AfO is supported in the new Cloud environment, AfO report broadcasting is not. As organizations move beyond SAP BusinessObjects, they are not seeing its Publications functionality replicated in the new environment.

APOS Publisher for Cloud provides enhanced report broadcasting for SAP Analytics Cloud, SAP Analytics Cloud Applications, and SAP Analysis for Office, extending the reach of your analytics to all stakeholders. It is an enterprise solution, meeting all of your SAP Analytics Cloud report scheduling and broadcasting needs.

APOS Publisher for Cloud removes barriers to SAC adoption so that more organizations can take full advantage of SAC’s reporting, visualization, planning and forecasting capabilities without compromising the scheduling and broadcasting workflows that their users depend on. This flexibility allows you to streamline your landscape and modernize your analytics.

Available on the SAP Store

entry.title: Story filter becomes open for user modification after being altered with script api
entry.content:
I have a filter in my story where I disabled "Allow viewers to modify selection" checkbox. 
Instead, I update this filter on a click of a button, with the script API similar to this:

 

var currentFilters = cast(Type.MultipleFilterValue, var_FDS.getDimensionFilters("CHANNEL")[0]);
	
currentFilters.values.push("another_channel");

var_FDS.setDimensionFilterWithHierarchy("CHANNEL", "HIER_1", currentFilters);

 

It works as expected (adding another channel to the channel filter), but after the execution the filter becomes editable by the users. As if the "Allow viewers to modify selection" checkbox was selected.

I think it might be a bug in SAP Analytics Cloud. Does anyone know how to avoid it?

entry.title: Re: DTP failed while loading the data from Query(as infop...
Re: Looping condition failing while polling files from SF...
entry.content:
Hi Ananya,

This is an expected behavior as far as I have noticed. There is an extra loop run which is due to the condition mentioned in the looping process call.

When you mention the condition as "${header.CamelFileName!='EC_Payroll*'", the name of the last file which was picked up will still be there in the header when the control goes back to looping process call and the condition will not be met. So an extra run will be there for which no file will be picked up.

To handle this, you can add a router condition based on the file name just after the poll enrich step and check if it is "EC_Payroll*" or not. If yes, then you can end the flow (or whatever you want the next step be) for this extra loop run.

Hope this helps. 

Regards,

Ankita Kandpal

entry.title: Re: DTP failed while loading the data from Query(as infop...
Re: POSC Config for Non-HU Product
entry.content:
Hi,

what is a "non-HU product"? You mean a product which is not stored in a HU?

You did the correct thing for LOSC, you created a pick-HU, as you wrote, then LOSC is working.

Now for POSC to work during the picking process - the process is determined with the warehouse order creation rule. So you need to check which WOCR is determined. If the one you expected is not found, I would recommend to activate the WO creation log and to check where the problem comes from.

Brgds

Juergen

---
All the above is no official SAP statement.

Want to learn EWM?
Check for EWM courses: https://training.sap.com/trainingpath/Applications-Extended+Warehouse+Management-EWM+in+S4HANA
Get a SAP Learning Hub Subscription: https://training.sap.com/learninghub

And it is EWM. NOT eWM - Duh!
And if your question includes the word "transfer order" - do NOT tag the question with Extended Warehouse Management!

Please check the configuration of your reclassification method also. There is a setting "remove predefined filters" which enables the selection of PCC data on posting level 02, 12, 22 also.

You may also need to enhance your trigger selection object by record type.

entry.title: Exciting Developments in the Auckland SAP Community: A Recap of the 2nd SAP Inside Track Event
entry.content:
A Fantastic Venue and Inspiring Talks

Deloitte generously hosted this exciting event at their new premises, which provided a perfect backdrop for a day of learning and networking. The venue was buzzing with nearly 32 participants who brought their enthusiasm and expertise to the discussions.

We were privileged to hear from two incredible speakers:

  • Luis Henrique Becker presented on "Demystifying the Clean Core Strategy for S/4HANA." His talk shed light on the complexities of the Clean Core approach, offering practical insights and strategies to navigate this crucial aspect of SAP S/4HANA.
  • Advit Ramesh discussed "Best Practices with Setting Up DevOps for SAP Integration Suite." Advit’s session provided a comprehensive overview of DevOps practices and their application within the SAP Integration Suite, helping attendees streamline their processes and enhance their integration capabilities.

What’s Next? Your Feedback Matters!

We deeply appreciate all who attended and contributed to the vibrant discussions. Your feedback has been incredibly valuable, and we’re excited to address the ideas and suggestions you shared. We heard requests for:

  • Extended discussions on functional areas like finance and sales.
  • More frequent events and opportunities for real problem-solving sessions.
  • Online participation options for those unable to attend in person.
  • Enhanced focus on AI and future SAP releases.
  • Better early advertising and practical elements like name tags.

We’re already looking forward to our next event, tentatively scheduled for the first week of December. We want to ensure it’s even more aligned with your interests and needs. If you have specific topics or areas you'd like us to cover, please don’t hesitate to reach out and share your suggestions.

Join the Community!

If you haven’t yet joined the Auckland SAP Community group, we warmly invite you to do so. It’s a fantastic way to stay connected, exchange knowledge, and keep up with upcoming events. Feel free to spread the word among your friends and colleagues in the SAP space—together, we can make this community even stronger.

Thank you once again to everyone who participated and contributed to the success of the 2nd SAP Inside Track - Auckland. We look forward to seeing you at future events and continuing our journey of learning and collaboration.

Stay tuned for more updates, and see you in December!
